summary: Today’s pulse tracks Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind, Australia politics live: Iranian women’s football squad member changes mind on asylum offer; embassies and consulate in Abu Dhabi, Dubai and Tel Aviv closed, and Iran war live updates: Regime issues threat against protesters; drone reportedly hits major US diplomatic facility in Iraq. Across BBC and Guardian, the strongest repeated signals are iranian, australia, and australian. In the background, Australians hit by soaring petrol prices now face expected blow of interest rate rise and Matt Canavan elected new Nationals leader and calls for ‘more Australian babies, more Australian everything’ keep the wider picture in motion.

Today’s pulse tracks Politics live: Canavan calls for ‘more Australian babies, more Australian everything’ after being elected Nationals leader, Democrat blasts Trump’s ‘incoherent’ Iran strategy after Pentagon says 140 US service members wounded in operation – live, and Middle East crisis live: Iran warns against protests, saying police have their ‘finger on the trigger’. Across The, the strongest repeated signals are continue, reading, and trump. In the background, ‘My lovely distraction’: Live stream of kākāpō – world’s fattest parrot – and her chicks captivates New Zealand and Naplan testing paused on first day for 1.4 million students amid widespread tech issues keep the wider picture in motion.

Today’s pulse tracks Middle East crisis live: Israel bombs Tehran and Beirut as Iran warns Europe to stay out of conflict or face ‘retaliation’, Trump says Cuba is ‘going to fall pretty soon’ after White House comments – live, and ICE arrests US Spanish-language news outlet reporter without warrant. Across The, the strongest repeated signals are continue, reading, and israel. In the background, Jesse Jackson hailed as ‘ambassador of hope’ at memorial attended by Clintons, Obama and Biden and Investors are expecting Donald Trump to back down in the war with Iran – but what if he doesn’t keep the wider picture in motion.
